summary:
A high infant mortality rate due to stillbirth and early neonatal death; calculation shows an approximate value around 196 per thousand based on provided figures for infants dying within one year from all recorded births.
The summary highlights critical aspects by focusing on specific causes contributing significantly to overall infant mortality rates while providing context through numerical analysis derived directly from stated conditions. $ Agent2 reasoned about calculating the infant mortality rate using specified numbers related primarily to newborn health outcomes such as stillbirths, those who did not survive beyond their first month or up until age one, then applied these values into standard formulas used clinically across populations similar demographically speaking like this rural setting with defined parameters over time frames pertinent specifically here being annual incidence metrics impacting public healthcare planning strategies effectively addressing core issues presented initially regarding survival statistics among young children therein.
